bugs.error_message: some players are missing positions now
bugs.root_cause: upsert_player_enrichment() never wrote position_code even though the landing API (/v1/player/{id}/landing) returns it. Affected 121 retired/inactive players whose only path into the players table was a stub (name + position only sometimes missing) later filled by enrichment, which silently skipped the position field. Same root-cause family as the earlier bio-data gap (missing enrichment fields), just for a different column.
bugs.fix: Added position_code = COALESCE(position_code, ?) to upsert_player_enrichment()'s UPDATE (fill-only, never overwrites a known value). Extracted data.get("position") in etl/enrich_players.py and passed it through. Added `OR position_code IS NULL` to the enrichment WHERE clause so previously-skipped players get picked up. Re-ran enrichment for the 121 affected players (0 errors) — confirmed 0/1776 players with null position_code afterward.

bugs.error_message: Search suggestion click silently fails to show the selected player (e.g. Nathan MacKinnon)
bugs.root_cause: selectSuggestion() only reset searchText before trying to scroll to the clicked player's row. It never reset activeTeam, activePositions, or statMins. If any of those three filters were still active from earlier browsing and excluded the selected player, render() never produced a <tr> for them, document.querySelector returned null, and the scroll-to-row guard (`if (!row) return;`) silently aborted — looking exactly like the player couldn't be found.
bugs.fix: Added clearOtherFilters() (resets activeTeam, activePositions, statMins and syncs their DOM controls: team popup button text, .pos-toggle active classes, .stat-input field values) and call it from selectSuggestion() before render(). searchText reset behavior unchanged.

bugs.error_message: pip-audit flagged known CVEs in pinned deps: requests 2.32.3 (PYSEC-2026-1872, CVE-2026-25645), flask 3.1.1 (CVE-2026-27205), pytest 8.3.5 (PYSEC-2026-1845)
bugs.root_cause: No dependency-audit tooling was gated in CI, so pinned versions silently aged past their patched releases with zero visibility (repo also had Dependabot alerts disabled — see project decision log).
bugs.fix: Bumped requests to 2.33.0, flask to 3.1.3, pytest to 9.0.3 (moved to requirements-dev.txt). Added scripts/audit.sh (pip-audit + bandit) as a required CI gate so future drift fails the PR instead of going unnoticed.

bugs.error_message: Appending `# nosec B608` on the same physical line as an opening f""" corrupts the SQL string instead of suppressing the bandit finding
bugs.root_cause: A Python comment cannot appear on a line that also opens a triple-quoted string literal — everything after f""" until the closing """ is string content, not code. `rows = conn.execute(f"""  # nosec B608` silently embedded "  # nosec B608" as literal text inside the SQL sent to SQLite. Caught by inspection before running anything, then confirmed two ways: bandit still reported the finding (0 skipped) proving the nosec never registered, and it would have broken the query at runtime.
bugs.fix: Extracted the f-string into a `query = f"""..."""` assignment so the closing `"""` and the `# nosec B608` comment could land on their own line, then called `conn.execute(query, seasons)` on the next line. Re-verified via py_compile + an end-to-end Flask test-client smoke test against a real SQLite DB.

bugs.error_message: Searching a full name (e.g. "Nathan MacKinnon") returns zero results, though last-name-only search ("MacKinnon") works
bugs.root_cause: renderSuggestions() and render()'s search filter both required the raw, unsplit query to be a substring of first_name OR last_name independently. A two-word query is longer than either field alone, so "nathan mackinnon" never matched "nathan" or "mackinnon" individually. Team names (team_name/team_abbrev) were never checked at all — team search was entirely unimplemented in the search box. Distinct from bug-002 (already-fixed filter-state bug on suggestion click) despite the same repro name.
bugs.fix: Extracted a shared, tested matchesQuery(player, query) helper into a new static/js/search.js (tokenizes the query on whitespace, requires every token to be found via AND semantics across a concatenated first_name+last_name+team_name+team_abbrev+team_place_name haystack). Order-independent by construction ("Nathan MacKinnon" and "MacKinnon Nathan" both work) and gives team-name search for free once team fields are in the haystack. Added t.place_name AS team_place_name to /api/players (extracted into a new testable _fetch_players(conn) helper in app.py) so "Colorado" matches alongside "Avalanche"/"COL". Added tests/js/search.test.js (13 cases, Node's built-in node:test, zero new deps) and a pytest case for _fetch_players. Superseded an earlier partial fix (PR #11) that only handled forward-order names with no team search and no tests.

bugs.error_message: Code review flagged two failure paths in scripts/launch_app.sh that could silently exit with no macOS alert dialog shown, defeating the launcher's core promise that any failure surfaces via osascript since there's no visible Terminal to read
bugs.root_cause: mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR" and source "$PROJECT_DIR/.venv/bin/activate" were not wrapped in their own if-guard + alert() the way every other risky step was, so under `set -euo pipefail` either one failing would exit the script immediately with no dialog. Separately, the `.venv` existence check only tested `[ -d .venv ]`, not that `.venv/bin/activate` actually existed, so a `.venv` left half-built by an interrupted prior run (crash, disk full, killed mid-creation) would skip re-creation and then fail silently at the unguarded `source` line. Also flagged: `is_up()`'s `curl -s -o /dev/null` treats any completed HTTP response (200, 404, 500) as "already running," the same class of false-positive health check that motivated the port 5000->5099 move in the first place.
bugs.fix: Added `trap 'alert "Unexpected error..."' ERR` as a catch-all so any unguarded command failure still shows a dialog. Changed the venv check to `[ ! -f "$PROJECT_DIR/.venv/bin/activate" ]` so a half-built venv gets recreated instead of silently failing at source. Changed `is_up()` to `curl -sf` so only an actual 2xx/3xx response counts as "up." Re-verified cold start, warm start, and (new) a deliberately broken requirements.txt to confirm the alert dialog fires correctly and the script exits non-zero rather than hanging or failing silently.

bugs.error_message: User double-clicked ~/Desktop/NHL Stats.app and nothing happened — no browser opened, no error dialog shown
bugs.root_cause: Two layers. (1) Expected/sequencing: PR #27 (adding scripts/launch_app.sh and the port-5099 app.py change) hadn't merged yet, so the compiled app's hardcoded absolute path (/Users/paulmckay/Desktop/NHL Stats Project/scripts/launch_app.sh) didn't exist on `main` at the time of testing. (2) Real design gap, empirically confirmed via an isolated osascript test: a bare `try / do shell script quoted form of "<path>" / end try` swallows ALL errors from `do shell script`, including "no such file" — not just the non-zero-exit case the try/end try was designed to suppress (where launch_app.sh's own osascript alert already fires). When do shell script can't even invoke the target (missing file, not executable, etc.), the user gets zero feedback: no dialog, no browser, nothing.
bugs.fix: Added a pre-flight `do shell script "test -x " & quoted form of scriptPath` check in launch_app.applescript before the real invocation. If that fails, an explicit `display dialog` fires naming the missing path, instead of relying on the bare try/end try to (incorrectly) let launch_app.sh's own error handling cover this case. Verified via an isolated /tmp osascript test that the on-error branch fires correctly for a missing/non-executable file and is a no-op for a real one, then re-verified the real compiled ~/Desktop/NHL Stats.app end-to-end (happy path: 200 response; failure path: script chmod -x'd, confirmed the app didn't silently start nothing).

bugs.error_message: Column header row renders displaced into the player row list (e.g. appearing as the 3rd row, between two data rows) instead of pinned at the top, on both Bio and Stats tabs; also does not stay pinned while scrolling.
bugs.root_cause: `.table-wrap` set only `overflow-x: auto`, leaving `overflow-y` unset. Per the CSS overflow spec, the browser computes the unset axis to `auto` too, making `.table-wrap` its own scroll container and the sticky-positioning containing block for `thead th`, instead of the page viewport. Since `.table-wrap` was never actually scrolled internally (the page itself scrolled via `<body>`), the `top: var(--thead-top)` sticky offset (~150-250px, the toolbar's real height) permanently displaced the header down by that amount from `.table-wrap`'s own top edge, even at scroll offset 0 — confirmed against a screenshot showing the header rendered as the 3rd row.
bugs.fix: Gave `.table-wrap` an explicit bounded height (`max(200px, calc(100vh - var(--toolbar-height, 57px)))`) and real `overflow: auto`, making it an intentional scroll pane. Changed `thead th`'s sticky `top` from `var(--thead-top)` to `0` (now correctly relative to `.table-wrap`'s own scrollport). Renamed the CSS var `--thead-top` to `--toolbar-height` to match its new purpose, and added a `resize` listener so the pane stays sized correctly. The 200px floor (added after code review) prevents the calc() from going negative and collapsing the pane to zero height on short viewports.

bugs.error_message: sqlite3.IntegrityError: FOREIGN KEY constraint failed when calling insert_game_event/insert_player_shift in the task-1-brief's own verbatim test code (test_insert_game_event_is_idempotent, test_insert_player_shift_is_idempotent)
bugs.root_cause: game_events.game_id and player_shifts.game_id are both `INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES games(game_id)`. The shared `conn` pytest fixture goes through database.get_connection(), which sets `PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON`, so FK enforcement is active in tests. The task-1-brief's test code stubs the player_id FK via ensure_player_stub()/_stub_player() but never inserts a games row for game_id=100 before inserting a game_events/player_shifts row referencing it — SQLite's `INSERT OR IGNORE` only suppresses UNIQUE/PK/NOT NULL/CHECK conflicts, not FOREIGN KEY violations (confirmed via an isolated repro), so the insert raises IntegrityError instead of being silently skipped.
bugs.fix: Added a `_stub_game(conn, game_id)` test helper in tests/test_database.py (mirrors the existing `_stub_player` convention) that calls database.insert_game() with a minimal games row (only game_date is NOT NULL in the games table; season_id/team ids can be None since NULL FK values are never checked). Called `_stub_game(conn, 100)` at the top of both idempotency tests before the game_events/player_shifts inserts. Schema and insert_game_event/insert_player_shift/ensure_player_stub implementations in src/database.py were NOT changed — this was purely a missing test-setup step.

bugs.error_message: sqlite3.IntegrityError: FOREIGN KEY constraint failed when calling insert_game_event from etl.load_play_by_play.run() in the task-4-brief's own verbatim idempotency test (test_run_does_not_duplicate_events_on_second_invocation)
bugs.root_cause: game_events.event_owner_team_id is `INTEGER REFERENCES teams(team_id)`, a separate FK from the game_id one bug-009 already covered. The task-4-brief's fake_plays test fixture sets `eventOwnerTeamId: 1` in the play details, which _extract_event carries through to event_owner_team_id=1 on the row passed to insert_game_event. The conn fixture's fresh test db (PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON) has no teams row for team_id=1 — unlike production, where load_teams.py always populates the teams table before load_play_by_play.py runs — so the FK check fails on the very first insert_game_event call inside run(). _ensure_referenced_players() only stubs player_id FKs (shooting/blocking/goalie/assist1/assist2) via database.ensure_player_stub(); there is no analogous ensure_team_stub() helper, and none was needed since only test data references an unseeded team.
bugs.fix: Added `database.upsert_team(conn, {"team_id": 1, "abbrev": "TST", "common_name": "Test", "place_name": "Test", "conference": None, "division": None})` as a setup step at the top of the idempotency test, before insert_game/run(). upsert_team already existed in src/database.py (used by etl/load_teams.py) and needed no changes. etl/load_play_by_play.py's production code was NOT changed — this was purely a missing test-setup step, same class of gap as bug-009 but on a different FK column.

bugs.error_message: npm run build fails: src/components/PositionToggle.tsx(27,18): error TS2322: Type '{ children: Element[]; type: string; value: string[]; }' is not assignable to type ... Property 'type' does not exist on type ...
bugs.root_cause: PositionToggle.tsx used `<ToggleGroup type="multiple" value={Array.from(active)}>`, a Radix ToggleGroup idiom (Radix needs `type` to pick single- vs multi-select mode). This project's shadcn primitive (frontend/src/components/ui/toggle-group.tsx) wraps @base-ui/react's ToggleGroup, not Radix's, and Base UI's ToggleGroupProps (frontend/node_modules/@base-ui/react/toggle-group/ToggleGroup.d.ts) has no `type` prop at all — it has value/defaultValue/onValueChange/disabled/orientation/loopFocus/multiple instead. The Task 7 review only ran Vitest (which doesn't type-check JSX prop shapes against Radix vs Base UI signatures), not `npm run build`/`tsc`, so the invalid prop slipped through. Verified via ToggleGroup.js/Toggle.js source that each item's pressed/on state derives purely from `groupContext.value.indexOf(itemValue) > -1` (i.e. the externally controlled `value` array), independent of Base UI's own `multiple` flag — so PositionToggle's existing pattern of driving all state via each ToggleGroupItem's onClick plus a controlled `value` array needed no further changes once `type` was removed.
bugs.fix: Deleted the `type="multiple"` prop from the `<ToggleGroup>` element in PositionToggle.tsx, leaving `<ToggleGroup value={Array.from(active)}>`. No other prop changes needed (confirmed Base UI's `multiple` flag only affects its own internal setGroupValue/onValueChange bookkeeping, not the read-side pressed-state derivation, which already comes from the controlled `value` array). `npm run build` now exits 0; `npm test` (vitest run) still passes all 28 tests across 5 files, including PositionToggle.test.tsx's 3 cases, unchanged.
